evidence_records.raw_text:
| Time step | O&M sample text |
|---|---|
| T1 | On Aurex BatteryHub-612 LR, record coolant level, residue color, undertray wetness, recent chiller work, and whether the seepage already points to the left coolant-plate outlet before any shield or hose support is moved. |
| T2 | Isolate the high-voltage pack, remove the local underbody shield, and expose the outlet quick connector, aluminum cooling-plate edge, hose support bracket, and nearby harness clip while keeping the hose angle unchanged so the original witness pattern remains readable. |
| T3 | Wipe only loose dirt, leave the wet boundary intact, and trace fresh coolant from the lowest drip point back toward the outlet tube bead, connector latch window, green O-ring land, and plate seam instead of assuming the largest stain marks the source. |
| T4 | Cap the neighboring branch, apply pressure to the suspect cooling section, and hold long enough to see whether new wetting starts at the connector shell, the O-ring interface, the hose end, or the aluminum plate seam beside the outlet neck. |
| T5 | Compare latch height, tube insertion depth, hose twist, and bracket side load with the opposite-side outlet branch, because a coupler body held off-axis by the support bracket often leaks while the plate seam stays dry. |
| T6 | Close the inspection only after the report names the exact leak boundary as coolant-plate seam, outlet O-ring seat, connector shell crack, or hose-end distortion, with the pressure result and residue path tied to that hardware. |

evidence_records.raw_text:
| Time step | O&M sample text |
|---|---|
| T1 | On Kestrel VMC-850, record whether the spindle chiller leak appears only under circulation or remains wet after shutdown, and note the exact hose branch, residue color, and cabinet-side drip path before the cover is opened. |
| T2 | Isolate machine power as required, expose the spindle chiller hoses, hose clamps, tube necks, and nearby cable tray, and preserve the as-found hose twist so the original side load can still be judged. |
| T3 | Wipe only loose coolant film, then trace the wet boundary from the lowest drip point back toward the hose neck, clamp line, quick connector, or chiller manifold seam instead of assuming the largest stain is the source. |
| T4 | Pressure-test the local cooling branch and hold long enough to see whether fresh coolant appears at the clamp gap, molded hose bend, tube bead, or manifold fitting, because tracking can blur the origin if the branch is released too early. |
| T5 | Compare clamp clocking, hose insertion depth, and bracket preload with a dry reference branch so a hose forced off-axis by the machine frame clip is not misread as a failed fitting. |
| T6 | Close the inspection only after the report names the exact leak boundary as upper spindle-chiller hose clamp, return-hose elbow, quick connector body, or manifold fitting, with the pressure result tied to that hardware. |
| T7 | Repeat the same speed, orientation, or clamp event after the local hardware settles so the accepted result reflects the true spindle operating condition rather than a one-time static check. |

concept_mentions.description: Repeat the same speed, orientation, or clamp event after the local hardware settles.

evidence_records.raw_text:
| Time step | O&M sample text |
|---|---|
| T1 | On vehicle Aurex E-Motion-412 LR, record battery coolant level, residue color, undertray wetness, recent thermal-system repair history, and whether seepage is already visible at the left coolant-plate outlet while the pack remains in the as-received condition with no covers moved. |
| T2 | Power the vehicle down, isolate the high-voltage pack, and allow the local cooling branch to equalize; then remove the rear underbody shield and the left pack-side splash cover while keeping the hose angle unchanged so the original quick-coupler seating witness can still be read. |
| T3 | Wipe only the loose surface film first, leave the wet edge untouched, and trace the leak path from the coolant-plate seam toward the outlet hose, the quick-coupler lock window, the O-ring seat, and the nearby bracket drain edge so the actual origin is not blurred by cleaning too early. |
| T4 | Install the local pressure tool on the coolant-plate inlet and outlet, cap the neighboring branch, and hold the specified pressure long enough to observe whether fresh wetting begins at the plate seam, the coupler body, the hose clamp line, or the tube insertion stop inside the connector. |
| T5 | Compare the suspect joint with the opposite coolant-plate branch, paying attention to hose twist, clamp clocking, connector latch height, tube insertion depth, and any side-load mark where the support bracket may have forced the plastic coupler body off-axis. |
| T6 | Inspect the aluminum plate edge, the molded quick connector, the green O-ring land, the hose end, and the nearby harness clip for dried coolant crystals, abrasion, plastic stress whitening, or metal staining that would separate connector leakage from a plate-side crack. |
| T7 | Mark the confirmed leak boundary directly at the leaking hardware, photograph the coolant-plate seam and the quick-coupler seat from more than one angle, and note clearly whether the failure belongs to the plate seam, the O-ring interface, the connector shell, or the hose-routing condition. |
| T8 | Refit only the access parts needed to protect the opened area, leave the identified leak point readable for downstream repair, and close the record with the exact hardware named as battery coolant-plate outlet quick connector or coolant-plate seam instead of leaving a generic leak description. |

concept_mentions.label: Aurex E-Motion-412 LR
concept_mentions.description: Vehicle model name.

concept_mentions.label: Battery Pack
concept_mentions.description: High-voltage battery pack.

concept_mentions.label: Coolant Plate
concept_mentions.description: Component for battery cooling, with seams and outlets.

concept_mentions.label: Outlet Hose
concept_mentions.description: Hose connected to the coolant-plate outlet.

concept_mentions.label: Quick Coupler
concept_mentions.description: Quick-connect fitting with lock window, O-ring seat, and body.

concept_mentions.label: O-ring
concept_mentions.description: Sealing ring within the quick coupler.

concept_mentions.label: Hose Clamp
concept_mentions.description: Clamp securing the hose.

concept_mentions.label: Support Bracket
concept_mentions.description: Bracket near the connector.

concept_mentions.label: Harness Clip
concept_mentions.description: Clip for nearby wiring harness.

concept_mentions.label: Rear Underbody Shield
concept_mentions.description: Protective shield under the vehicle.

concept_mentions.label: Left Pack-Side Splash Cover
concept_mentions.description: Cover on the side of the battery pack.

concept_mentions.label: Local Pressure Tool
concept_mentions.description: Tool for pressure testing the cooling system.

concept_mentions.label: T1 Record Initial Condition
concept_mentions.description: Record battery coolant level, residue, wetness, history, and visible seepage.

concept_mentions.label: T2 Power Down and Gain Access
concept_mentions.description: Power down vehicle, isolate HV pack, remove shields and covers.

concept_mentions.label: T3 Wipe and Trace Leak Path
concept_mentions.description: Wipe surface film, trace leak path from plate seam to outlet components.

concept_mentions.label: T4 Pressure Test
concept_mentions.description: Install pressure tool, cap branch, hold pressure to observe fresh wetting.

concept_mentions.label: T5 Compare Suspect Joint
concept_mentions.description: Compare joint with opposite branch, noting hose twist, clamp clocking, latch height, etc.

concept_mentions.label: T6 Inspect Components
concept_mentions.description: Inspect plate edge, connector, O-ring land, hose end, and clip for defects.

concept_mentions.label: T7 Mark and Document Leak
concept_mentions.description: Mark leak boundary, photograph, note failure location.

concept_mentions.label: T8 Refit and Close Record
concept_mentions.description: Refit access parts, leave leak point readable, close record with exact hardware name.

concept_mentions.label: As-Received Condition
concept_mentions.description: Initial state of the vehicle/pack with no covers moved.

concept_mentions.label: Safe State
concept_mentions.description: State after powering down and isolating the high-voltage pack.

concept_mentions.label: Leak Path
concept_mentions.description: Observed path of coolant from origin to drain point.

concept_mentions.label: Fresh Wetting
concept_mentions.description: Observation of new coolant seepage during pressure test.

concept_mentions.label: Hose Twist
concept_mentions.description: Observed condition of hose orientation.

concept_mentions.label: Clamp Clocking
concept_mentions.description: Observed orientation/position of the hose clamp.

concept_mentions.label: Connector Latch Height
concept_mentions.description: Measured height of the connector latch.

concept_mentions.label: Tube Insertion Depth
concept_mentions.description: Measured depth of tube insertion into the connector.

concept_mentions.label: Side-Load Mark
concept_mentions.description: Mark indicating off-axis force on the coupler body.

concept_mentions.label: Dried Coolant Crystals
concept_mentions.description: Residue indicating past leakage.

concept_mentions.label: Abrasion
concept_mentions.description: Sign of wear on a component.

concept_mentions.label: Plastic Stress Whitening
concept_mentions.description: Discoloration indicating stress in plastic components.

concept_mentions.label: Metal Staining
concept_mentions.description: Staining on metal components.

concept_mentions.label: Leak
concept_mentions.description: General fault of coolant escaping from the system.

concept_mentions.label: Connector Leakage
concept_mentions.description: Fault: leak originating from the quick connector assembly.

concept_mentions.label: Plate-Side Crack
concept_mentions.description: Fault: crack in the coolant plate itself.
